display_name: Sky partition and cutout overlap validation
provenance_notes: First-party astronomy validation rule using source coordinates, image support, and acquisition groups; no external source code is copied.
checks: Declare the sky-coordinate frame and angular units used by the overlap audit.
Report exact source duplication separately from angular cutout overlap.
Compare the nearest cross-partition angular separation with the declared cutout support.
Report shared field, tile, exposure, or acquisition groups across partitions.
Use grouped or blocked validation when broad spatial or acquisition generalization is claimed.
Do not call a split leakage-free when only identifier duplication was tested.

rules: The angular-overlap radius must come from the declared image support, not a universal constant.
No universal performance threshold is imposed on grouped validation; report the random-versus-grouped difference and uncertainty.

threshold_source.citation_or_note: Cutout size, coordinate frame, and acquisition grouping are project-specific evidence.

review_question: Do the declared source and sky partitions support the manuscript's leakage and generalization claims?
scientific_risk: Identifier-only checks can miss overlapping cutouts, nearby sources, and shared acquisition context across train and evaluation partitions.
